items.title: Enforce game directory rules: disable mod management if no dir set
items.detail: If a game has no directory configured, the mod manager must not attempt management. Instead, show a clear prompt to set the folder. Currently, conflicting messaging about deployment appears rather than a simple 'set folder first' reminder. This guardrail should gate all mod management actions (install, deploy, etc.).
items.notes: Phase 1 â€” must-fix before deployment UX makes sense. Install button now guards on GameDirectory; deploy already guarded via _pendingCustom.

items.title: Fix UI lag: 'Needs folder' state persists too long after path is set
items.detail: After the user sets a game directory, game cards and workspace indicators continue showing a 'needs folder' state for longer than expected. The UI should update promptly (ideally immediately) when the path is confirmed.
items.notes: Phase 1 â€” minor but noticeable friction on first run. UpdatePathAffordances + UpdateSidebarCustom now called immediately in BtnBrowseGameFolder_Click before the async refresh.

items.title: Remove the Quit button from the rail
items.detail: BtnQuit_Click in UnifiedShellWindow.xaml:178. The button moved from 'bottom-left corner' (old layout) to the bottom of the global rail but the original request was to remove it entirely.

items.title: Enable window resizing
items.detail: UnifiedShellWindow uses WindowStyle=None with no WndProc resize-region override. MinWidth/MinHeight are set but dragging the edge does nothing. Needs hit-test override in TmmWindow.cs or UnifiedShellWindow.xaml.cs.

items.title: Fix double-click titlebar maximize
items.detail: TmmWindow.TitleBar_MouseDown calls DragMove() only; no MouseDoubleClick handler. BtnMaximize_Click toggle works via button but titlebar double-click does not.
items.notes: When double-clicking, the window briefly maximizes before returning to its' original size in a split second.

items.title: Restrict address bar width in Downloads tab
items.detail: If the WebView2 browser address bar is visible in the Downloads workspace tab, it should not span full width. Verify visibility first, then constrain MaxWidth if shown.

items.title: Welcome screen: lead with value, demote language picker
items.detail: The welcome screen currently leads with a language dropdown â€” the first thing a new user sees. Should lead with a 'Go to your library' primary action and a brief value prop. Language picker moves to a corner control (it already exists in the status bar).
items.notes: Distinct from the first-time wizard revamp (see firstrun section). This is the splash/landing screen; the wizard is what runs when adding a first game.

items.title: Right-click / â‹¯ overflow discoverability
items.detail: Set Group, Set Color, Set Artwork, Export, Archive all live in the right-click/overflow menu. Power-user-friendly, but a new user may never find them. Consider a first-time tooltip, subtle indicator, or onboarding hint.

items.title: Implement sidebar navigation (Logo, Library, Mod Manager, Help, Settings)
items.detail: Decision #6: Vertical rail on left. Top-aligned: Logo (themed icon, renders with current theme color), Library (Books icon), Mod Manager (wrench icon). Bottom-aligned: Help & Troubleshooting (merges FAQ, log viewer, About), Settings (app paths and preferences). Replaces old top menu bar. Additionally: make the sidebar resizable. Add a hamburger icon toggle â€” goes in the exact same position where the current Install Mod button sits (which is moving to the action bar per Decision #8). The hamburger collapses/expands the sidebar.
items.notes: This is more of a change to the existing vertical bar than something entirely new. Resizable + hamburger toggle are Phase 4 additions to this item.

items.title: Consolidate filter searchbox into tab row
items.detail: Decision #7: Merge filter searchbox + tab row (All / Enabled / Conflicts / Favorites) into a single horizontal control in mod manager. Removes dedicated search element to reclaim vertical space.

items.title: Move Install Mod button inline with Deploy and Play
items.detail: Decision #8: Install Mod button moves to action bar alongside Deploy and Play buttons at bottom of mod manager. The vacated position is taken by the sidebar hamburger toggle (see ui-sidebar-structure).

items.title: Custom dark mode scrollbar for all scrollable areas
items.detail: Default Windows light-mode scrollbars cause two problems: (1) they are visually inconsistent with the dark theme, and (2) they shift centered UI elements slightly left when visible. Apply a custom dark scrollbar style to all scrollable tabs/panels. Priority areas: Library tab and Notifications window. Not needed for the mod list (separate concern).
items.notes: Phase 4. Cosmetic but noticeable â€” centered elements drifting left is especially annoying.

items.title: Browse window QoL: show game name in title bar
items.detail: When the user opens the browse-for-game-directory dialog, include the game's name in the window title bar so the user knows which game they are setting the path for at a glance.
items.notes: Phase 3. Already implemented: OpenFolderDialog title = 'Select {GameName} Folder'.

items.title: Status bar: add disk space indicator and game selection display
items.detail: Status bar currently shows language selector only. Add secondary functions: disk free space for selected game directory, and/or indicator of which game workspace is active.
items.notes: You can add other stuff to the status bar as applicable.

items.title: Library scroll indicators: circular arrows for overflow content
items.detail: Show a circular down arrow when content continues below the visible area, and a circular up arrow when content continues above. Applies to the Library tab only â€” not the mod list, not the notifications list. Purely decorative/UX; does not replace the scrollbar.
items.notes: Phase 3. Prettiness specifically for the library; not a global pattern.

items.title: Expose .tmmgame filenames in small text on library cards
items.detail: Display the backing .tmmgame filename in small, subtle text on each game card in the library. Conceptually, the library is a polished file browser for .tmmgame files â€” exposing filenames reinforces this and helps power users identify profiles.
items.notes: Phase 3. SourceFileName added to CustomGameProfile (JsonIgnore); populated in GameRegistry for both built-in .tmmgame and custom .json profiles. TmmGameFileName added to LibraryEntry. txtTmmGameFile / listTxtTmmGameFile shown on GameCard in 8.5pt/9.5pt semi-transparent text.

items.title: Library context menus: open Config editor directly from game card
items.detail: Right-click (or â‹¯ overflow) on a game card in the library should include an option to open the config editor (game wizard in edit mode) directly, without needing to navigate into the Mod Manager workspace first.
items.notes: Phase 3. Pairs with library-path-setting.

items.title: Quick path setting from library: set game dir without entering the wizard
items.detail: Game directory should be settable directly from the library view â€” e.g. via card context menu or a dedicated 'Set path' control â€” without needing to open the full wizard or navigate into the Mod Manager. This is important for second, third, etc. games after the first-run wizard has already been completed. Open question: does this also inform the design of the Config tab (see nav-config-tab-justify)?
items.notes: Phase 3. Closely related to firstrun-wizard-revamp. Design decision pending on whether path-setting separates from the full wizard flow entirely.

items.title: Merge Help + Troubleshooting into single global destination
items.detail: Decision #3: Unified 'Help & Troubleshooting' rail entry. Content: FAQ, log/error viewer, About. Replaces the separate Troubleshooting rail entry and the Help/About overflow items. Always accessible regardless of which workspace is active.
items.notes: Rail button tooltip and titlebar text updated to 'Help & Troubleshooting'. Still navigates to the existing TroubleshootingPage â€” a proper unified Help+About+Log content page is out of scope for this pass.

items.title: Merge File Locations (Paths) into Settings
items.detail: Paths is currently a separate rail entry. The request is to absorb it into the Settings page so the rail shrinks. Explicitly deferred in M1 but still open.
items.notes: Paths button removed from rail. File Locations section added to SettingsPage (settingsPathRowsPanel, BuildPathRows helper). Navigating to 'Paths' via code highlights Settings in the rail.

items.title: Config tab: quick path-setting + 'Advanced config' link to wizard
items.detail: DECIDED: The Config tab is a lightweight panel, not a full wizard launch. It shows a quick path-setting control (browse + confirm game directory) and an 'Advanced config' link/button that opens the full wizard in edit mode. This updates Decision #4. Relates to library-path-setting (same quick-path pattern, different surface).
items.notes: Phase 3. Decision locked in â€” no longer an open question.

items.title: Settings: explicit Save / Apply / Cancel flow
items.detail: SettingsPage currently auto-saves on every change. No Save/Apply/Cancel flow. This causes notification/log spam from accidental adjustments. Needs unsaved-changes prompt on navigate away.
items.notes: It is nice not to force the user into the option right away, but maybe the color options should auto-apply before hitting Apply, might be annoying if you're testing out some color combos iteratively.

items.title: Notifications page design review
items.detail: The Notifications page (bell icon in rail) was added in v0.1-alpha-10. No design review has been done â€” content layout, empty state, and interaction model have not been audited.
items.notes: Consider replacing notifications with notification history with a clock icon instead of noti bell.

items.title: Revamp first-time setup wizard: .tmmgame dropdown + path-first flow
items.detail: Occurs after the welcome screen (see ux-welcome-screen) â€” sequential flows, not alternatives. The current first-game setup wizard is 13 steps â€” far too long for most users. New flow: Step 1 = select a bundled .tmmgame file from a dropdown (the list of included profiles); Step 2 = set the game directory path. Most users will not be building custom game profiles from scratch. The lengthy custom-game steps should only appear when the user explicitly opts into 'Add custom game.' Path-setting for additional games after the first must be discoverable from the library (see library-path-setting) without re-entering the wizard.
items.notes: Phase 2. Wizard auto-launches after the welcome screen â€” no manual navigation required for first run. Second-game path discovery is handled by library-path-setting (library card context menu). Related to games-wizard-unified.

items.title: Startup preference: Library first or Mod Manager first
items.detail: Let the user choose whether TMM opens to the Library or the Mod Manager on launch. If Mod Manager: go to the default game if one is set, otherwise fall back to last-managed game. If Library: ensure the default game (if set) appears first in the list. Preference should be exposed in Settings and ideally during or shortly after first run.
items.notes: Phase 2. Pairs with firstrun-default-game. AppSettings.StartupPage added; radio buttons in Settings page; Window_Loaded respects the preference.

items.title: Default game handling: expose control, fall back to last-opened
items.detail: Empower users to set a default game during and after first launch. No need to prompt or force the choice â€” just expose the control (e.g. in the library game card context menu or a star/pin icon). When no default is set, automatically track the last-opened game and show it first. The default indicator should be easily visible and settable from the library.
items.notes: Phase 2. Infrastructure already existed end-to-end (ActiveGameKey, IsActive, star button on GameCard, ActiveToggled event). No new code needed â€” fully functional.

items.title: Notify user when the default game changes
items.detail: If the user already has a default game set and sets a new one, show a notification: 'Default changed from [Game A] to [Game B].' If no previous default existed, the notification should just confirm what the new default is. Standard notification channel â€” no modal.
items.notes: Phase 2. OnActiveToggled in UnifiedShellWindow.xaml.cs now shows clear/set/change notifications.

items.title: Fix tab navigation: Mods / Conflicts / Backups / Downloads / Config
items.detail: PRIORITY: Tabs currently cannot be clicked at all â€” switching between workspace tabs is completely broken. Fix this first before any other mod list work. All five tabs must be accessible and switch cleanly. Conflicts tab displays ConflictAnalyzer results; Backups lists per-deploy rollback points; Downloads shows mod download history; Config opens game wizard in edit mode.
items.notes: Phase 1 â€” blocking all other mod manager functionality. Root cause: MouseLeftButtonDown=TitleBar_MouseDown on the master window Border caused DragMove() to fire on every click, consuming the MouseLeftButtonUp event before it reached the tab handlers. Fixed by removing that handler from the master border â€” titlebar row already has its own.

items.title: Rename 'Conflicts' tab to 'Conflict Manager'
items.detail: The main Mods tab should show whether conflicts exist at a glance. The dedicated tab is for actively managing or resolving them â€” the name 'Conflict Manager' reflects that purpose and sets clearer expectations for users.
items.notes: Phase 4. Simple rename. Updated en-US.json and es-MX.json.

items.title: Ghost checkbox artifact on hover â€” verify resolved
items.detail: M2 replaced the GridView column layout with a full custom ItemTemplate DataTemplate. The ghost checkbox was a hover-state artifact of the old template. Needs visual verification in the running app to confirm it's gone.

items.title: Double-click Order field for inline editing
items.detail: The M2 DataTemplate shows a numeric order index. No double-click inline-edit handler exists. Requested: type a value directly, all other entries shift to accommodate.

items.title: Multi-attribute column-header sorting + tiering
items.detail: Restore and expand sorting: support load order, mod name, mod group, isEnabled, size, status. Sorting must support ascending and descending for each column. Modlist must be tiered again (visual grouping by mod group or type). Column headers should be clickable to sort; clicking the active column header toggles direction. Pairs with the up/down arrow re-ordering controls in modlist-entry-controls.
items.notes: Phase 4. Tiering re-add is required alongside sort controls.

items.title: Per-mod entry controls: folder icon, up/down arrows, re-install
items.detail: Add controls to each mod row in the mod list: (1) Folder icon â€” opens the mod's stored folder in Explorer. (2) Up/down arrows â€” manually re-order load order; should also respond to keyboard arrow keys when a mod row is focused. (3) Re-install / Edit Plan â€” two sub-paths: (a) Archive still present â†’ ask user if they want to re-import from the archive (warn that manual changes to the mod folder will be overwritten) or just open the Plan Editor to adjust the existing plan. (b) Archive gone â†’ inform user the archive is no longer present; skip re-import option; open the Plan Editor (install-preview) directly so the user can re-plan the already-extracted mod files without a full re-install. In all destructive paths, show a confirmation dialog before proceeding.
items.notes: Phase 4. Architecturally significant. The 'pre-deployment UI' for the archive-gone branch is the expanded DeployPreviewWindow / Deployment Plan Editor (see install-preview).

items.title: Empty mod list state
items.detail: When a game has no mods, the Mods workspace tab is blank. Needs a gentle empty-state: illustration or 'drag a mod here / browse Downloads' prompt to reduce friction for new users.

items.title: Wire up Favorites support: toggle and filter
items.detail: Favorites tab (subset of enabled mods) exists in the filter chips but backend support and visual toggles are incomplete. Add star/heart icon to each mod row to mark as favorite. Favorites chip filters to this subset. Persist favorite status in mod metadata.

items.title: Conflict Manager tab: ConflictAnalyzer integration and readability
items.detail: Ensure ConflictAnalyzer surfaces all cross-mod file overlaps and display is readable (grouped by conflict set, showing affected files and involved mods). This is the Conflict Manager tab (previously 'Conflicts' â€” see modlist-tab-rename-conflicts). Additionally: when any conflict exists (detected during install, re-plan, or deploy), show a persistent notification bubble/badge on the Conflict Manager tab to draw user attention. Bubble persists until â€” see tracker.md Q15 for open question on when it clears.

items.title: Loadouts: deferred to long-term backlog (remove from UX)
items.detail: Loadouts exist in backend (%APPDATA%\TMM\Loadouts_{key}\) but are not fully backend-implemented for this release cycle. Remove loadout UX (switcher, save/load/delete) from current mod manager. Add to long-term feasibility study for future release. Depends on backend storage and load-order reconciliation.

items.title: [WON'T IMPLEMENT] Direct Install Override
items.detail: DECIDED: Not implementing. The routing rules system already supports a 'game root' destination â€” users who want a direct copy (e.g. dxvk) just need a mod type rule that targets the game directory root. A cleanly routed mod can be uninstalled, tracked, and conflict-checked. A special CTRL override mode with no DeploymentPlan, no clean uninstall, and a separate code path adds more complexity than value. Ensure 'game root / game directory' is a clearly visible and obvious routing destination option so this use case is naturally covered.
items.notes: Won't implement. Routing rules + 'game root' destination is the right answer.

items.title: Add .tar.gz archive support for mod installation
items.detail: Edge case: some mods (e.g. dxvk) ship as .tar.gz archives. Add .tar.gz to the supported archive formats for mod installation.
items.notes: Phase 5. Small scope â€” just extend the archive extraction handler.

items.title: Deployment Plan Editor: two-pane file levelling / routing UI
items.detail: Expand DeployPreviewWindow into a full two-pane deployment plan editor. FLOW: archive extracted to ModsRaw â†’ routing rules auto-populate DeploymentPlan as default â†’ Plan Editor opens â†’ user confirms or adjusts â†’ Confirm freezes plan / Cancel aborts install entirely. Always mandatory â€” shown on every install regardless of whether issues exist. LEFT PANE (game dir): read-only file tree; conflicts against ALL installed mods (enabled or disabled) highlighted visually; users CAN create folders (created on disk at deploy time); Explorer quick-link. RIGHT PANE (ModsRaw source layout): inline destination annotation per file (routing-rule assignment); drag file â†’ drop onto left-pane folder to reassign (annotation updates in place). Operations: reassign destination, create custom folders, rename files (renames in ModsRaw immediately), exclude from plan (stays in ModsRaw, not deployed). No drag-to-add files â€” use Explorer quick-link. UNRECOGNIZED FILE RULES (in order): (1) same dir level as recognized file â†’ carry to same destination; (2) filename contains 'readme' â†’ rename to readme_{modname}.txt, game root; (3) otherwise â†’ game root, flagged. CONFLICT BUBBLE: badge on Conflict Manager tab; clears only when all conflicts resolved. ORPHANED FILES: pre-deploy blocking dialog lists files no longer in plan, asks permission to remove before proceeding. RENAME: renames file in ModsRaw immediately. BATCH: sequential, alphabetical; mid-sequence cancel aborts that mod only, rest continue without prompt. CONFLICT SCOPE: all installed mods (enabled or disabled). EDIT EXISTING PLAN: accessible from mod list at any time; flags mod 'needs redeploy.' ARCHIVE-GONE: opens Plan Editor directly to re-plan extracted files.
items.notes: Significant feature. Full spec in tracker.md 'Deployment Plan Editor' section. All design questions resolved.

items.title: Strict duplicate mod validation
items.detail: No checksum + filename + size deduplication at install time. A user can install the same mod twice under different archive names.
items.notes: I don't think anything will really break if duplicates are found... maybe we should just warn the user, or ignore it.

items.title: Non-English character artifact in deployment notification
items.detail: Corrupted characters in the deployment toast string ("ace"-like). Not confirmed fixed. Needs a deploy run to observe the notification string live.

items.title: Remove built-in vs custom game distinction (architectural)
items.detail: Decision #5: All games are first-class. CustomGameProfile already has both IsNative and IsBuiltIn flags â€” overlapping and confusing. GameRegistry._builtInGames + _customGames are two dicts that should collapse into one. Remove all code paths that branch on 'is this a custom game'. Built-in games are just pre-seeded profiles from .tmmgame assets.

items.title: Rename CustomGameProfile â†’ GameConfig (or GameDefinition)
items.detail: CustomGameProfile is the full editable config model. The name is misleading once all games are unified. Rename across all 27+ files. The lightweight display record GameProfile stays or gets renamed to GameEntry/GameSummary to avoid ambiguity.

items.title: Replace CUSTOM_N key format with clean name-based slugs
items.detail: AddCustomGameAsync generates CUSTOM_1, CUSTOM_2, etc. (GameRegistry.cs:202). Replace with a slug derived from the game name (e.g. 'My Game' â†’ 'MyGame', deduplicated if needed). No CUSTOM_ prefix. Must handle existing installations via migration.

items.title: Clean mod folder names (ModsRaw_slug, not ModsRaw_CUSTOM_1)
items.detail: Mod folder paths derive directly from the game key. Once keys are slugified, folders follow automatically. But existing CUSTOM_N folders need migration: rename on disk + update any stored paths in deployplan.json, manifests, etc.

items.title: Rename CustomGameSetupWizard â†’ GameSetupWizard, remove built-in guards
items.detail: Decision #4: The wizard is the add + edit flow for every game, with no special cases for built-in profiles. Remove any UI branches that hide or disable fields for built-in games. Rename the wizard class and XAML file.

items.title: Redesign wizard: merge mod types + routing rules into one step
items.detail: Decision #2: Steps 2 and 3 are redundant. Combine into a single step where each row is a mod type with its file extensions and target folder. Decision #4 means the redesign must work for both add and edit flows for all games.

items.title: Implement edit mode: Config tab opens wizard for any game
items.detail: Decision #4: The Config tab in each game workspace opens the wizard in edit mode. This must work for built-in games (III, VC, SA, etc.) and user-added games identically. Changes persist back to the game's config file.

items.title: Consolidate GameRegistry: collapse two dicts into one
items.detail: GameRegistry currently has _builtInGames (static GameProfile records) and _customGames (CustomGameProfile + GameProfile pairs). Once built-in .tmmgame profiles fully replace static records and all games are unified, collapse to a single dict. Remove GetBuiltInGames(), GetCustomGames(), GetBuiltInCustomGames() â€” replace with GetAllGames() and optional tag-based filtering.

items.title: Dynamic scaling for long translated strings
items.detail: No adaptive layout logic for translated strings that exceed English lengths. Needs a pass across controls with fixed widths.
items.notes: Try not to go too far down the rabbit hole on this one.. we'll just use nice comfy buttons & whatnot.

items.title: Fix specific localization strings (verify in running app)
items.detail: Known issues: (1) Truncated: 'table differs from this profile's expe--' (2) Pluralization: '1 mods installed' (library stats strip) (3) es-MX: '1 mod - 1 enabled - last deployed 8 min ago' (4) 'app data' and 'free space' in Home view (5) 'import' and 'loadouts' in M1 overflow/tab-bar positions (verify they still exist) (6) All window titles (7) All color accent preset identifiers.

items.title: Add Opus 4.8 to AboutWindow credits
items.detail: AboutWindow.xaml:135 shows 'Claude Opus 4.7'. Opus 4.8 needs to be appended to the AI-assisted credits tag list.

items.title: Cold-start walkthrough: factory reset â†’ full install cycle
items.detail: Full live test: factory reset (or fresh AppData) â†’ library â†’ set game folder â†’ browse Downloads â†’ install a mod â†’ deploy â†’ verify game launches. Should be done before any public release. Catches issues the test suite cannot (missing files, first-run UX gaps, toast encoding).

items.title: Periodic module-size audit â€” flag files over ~800 lines
items.detail: Re-run when files sprawl. Last pass 2026-05-29 split BackendCore.cs and ModManagerPage.xaml.cs into per-concern partials. Next: re-inventory the top-20 largest source files, flag anything over ~800 lines for a partial-class split. WPF split gotcha: keep partial class + x:Class intact; never move InitializeComponent.

items.title: Loadouts: backend storage and load-order reconciliation
items.detail: Save/load mod configurations with full load-order preservation and conflict resolution. Blocked on backend storage design and LoadOrderResolver integration. Candidate for post-v1 release.

items.title: Virtual File System (VFS) feasibility assessment
items.detail: Assess technical feasibility and maintenance overhead of adding a mandatory VFS layer (MO2-parity). Consider: impact on DeploymentPlan-freeze architecture, performance on large mod sets, rollback complexity. Design note: if VFS is re-implemented, the Backup tab could be replaced with archive/download management for existing mod archives; existing Downloads tab becomes 'Downloader.' VFS would remove the need for the backup system and strengthen the purpose of per-mod isolated install paths.

items.title: Nexus Mods API integration feasibility assessment
items.detail: Assess feasibility of native NexusMods API support (mod browsing, metadata, download queue). Consider: API key management, rate limits, per-game category mapping. Also covers the deferred 'mod source + version line' and update-available badge from the M2 backport. Design note: Nexus API credentials/login should live in the Settings menu, not a dedicated rail entry.

items.title: FOMOD installer schema feasibility assessment
items.detail: Assess feasibility of parsing and presenting FOMOD ModuleConfig.xml during the pre-installation deployment preview. Consider: schema coverage, conditional install steps, interaction with DeploymentPlan freeze.

items.title: Single-layer undo for deploy actions feasibility assessment
items.detail: Assess feasibility of a single-level undo for deploy-time destructive actions, specifically orphaned file removal during smart partial redeploy. Consider: interaction with existing backup/rollback system (first-touch baseline + per-deploy manifests), storage overhead, UX surface (undo button in action bar vs notification toast). Does this supplement or replace the existing backup tab rollback?
items.notes: Deferred. Triggered by orphaned-file cleanup in smart partial redeploy.

items.title: Mod preview and image gallery support
items.detail: Display mod preview images, screenshots, or carousel in mod details. Requires optional mod metadata fields and preview asset storage/handling.
